default_creds.context: First-run admin auto-seeded by UserManagement.__init__ from KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN/_PASSWORD (both default 'admin' in flowsettings.py). README states verbatim: 'Default username and password are both admin.' No forced rotation, no rate limit on the login form.

install_tell: docker run -e GRADIO_SERVER_NAME=0.0.0.0 -e GRADIO_SERVER_PORT=7860 -v ./ktem_app_data:/app/ktem_app_data -p 7860:7860 -it --rm ghcr.io/cinnamon/kotaemon:main-full  (or: git clone https://github.com/Cinnamon/kotaemon && cd kotaemon && uv sync --python 3.10 && source .venv/bin/activate && python app.py)
misconfig_patterns: Default admin/admin left unchanged and port 7860 exposed to the internet -> effectively unauthenticated takeover (no forced first-run rotation, no rate limit)
KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT=false explicitly disabled -> fully open UI+API (operator opt-out turns it open-by-default)
KH_DEMO_MODE=true on a public deploy -> auto-creates random users and bypasses the login wall
Running < 0.10.7 -> CVE-2025-53358 arbitrary file read for any logged-in (incl. default-cred) user
Provider API keys committed to .env on the persisted ktem_app_data volume

response_markers: Gradio config JSON at /config includes a 'login-tab'/'Welcome' tab and version field (app is a Gradio Blocks app)
page references ktem assets path libs/ktem/ktem/assets
Gradio app_id / __theme markers present (X-Gradio or queue/join route answers)
login form posting to Gradio fn validating against sha256 hashed password
active_probe.false_positive_check: Generic 'RAG'/'chat'/'AI' strings collide with thousands of unrelated hosts and Gradio alone fronts countless HF Spaces, so do NOT fingerprint on Gradio or 'chat' alone. Require the conjunction of a Gradio app (component:Gradio or /config returning Gradio JSON) AND a vendor-unique kotaemon marker: the literal 'kotaemon' in <title> or page body, OR the 'ktem_app_data'/'libs/ktem/ktem/assets' path string, OR the kotaemon favicon hash. The presence of a 'login-tab' with the 'Welcome' label plus 'kotaemon' branding in the same Gradio /config JSON is the discriminator that strips the ~50% generic-Gradio false positives. A bare Gradio host with no 'kotaemon'/'ktem' token is a false positive.

deployment_tells: 'Welcome'/login-tab as the first Gradio tab when user management is on
OSS RAG product branded 'kotaemon' by Cinnamon AI
pivot_paths: GET /config -> read the Gradio app graph (tabs, fn signatures, version) to confirm kotaemon and enumerate callable fns without exfil
Confirm login wall by observing the 'Welcome' login tab is the only visible tab pre-auth (UI-state read, no creds submitted)
Inventory exposed upstream-provider env keys referenced in .env.example to scope which LLM vendor keys a compromise would burn (metadata, not the keys themselves)
Map version banner from page/body to scope CVE-2025-53358 exposure (< 0.10.7) without exploiting it
vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-53358 / GHSA-jw4w-xcvf-jq5x: Path Traversal via Link Upload in libs/ktem/ktem/index/file/ui.py index_fn() — accepts URLs and local file paths without validation, letting an authenticated (low-priv) user read arbitrary server files like ../../../../../.env. CVSS 6.5 Moderate. Affected: < 0.10.7. Chains with default admin/admin to become unauth-equivalent in practice.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: flowsettings.py L95-97 sets `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT = config("KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT", default=True, cast=bool)` -> user management ON by default. libs/ktem/ktem/main.py gates the UI: the Chat/Files/Settings tabs are rendered with `visible=not self.f_user_management` and `toggle_login_visibility` hides every tab except the login tab while `not user_id`; a LoginPage tab is added first when `f_user_management` is true. libs/ktem/ktem/pages/resources/user.py `UserManagement.__init__` auto-seeds the admin on first run: `usn = flowsettings.KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN; pwd = flowsettings.KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_PASSWORD; create_user(usn, pwd)` (create_user is a no-op if the user already exists). flowsettings.py defaults both to "admin": `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN` default="admin", `KH_FEATURE_USER_MANAGEMENT_PASSWORD` default="admin". NET POSTURE: the documented quickstart (`docker run ... ghcr.io/cinnamon/kotaemon:main-full` or `python app.py`) forces a login wall before any chat/document access, so it is auth-on-default, NOT open-by-default. The weakness is the credential, not the absence of a gate: the publicly-documented README states "Default username and password are both `admin`", with NO forced first-run password change and NO rate limiting in login.py (sha256-no-salt, DB lookup, gr.Warning on fail). KH_DEMO_MODE (default=False) would bypass login by auto-creating random users, but is off in the default config and so does not affect default posture.
sensitive_assets: Ingested RAG corpus: every uploaded document (the user's private knowledge base) readable/queryable post-login
Upstream LLM/provider API keys: OpenAI/Azure/Cohere/Mistral/Voyage/GraphRAG keys live in .env and the in-UI Resources/Settings tab, recoverable once logged in
Conversation history: per-user chat threads, including public/shared collections
Vector + full-text index contents (the embedded document chunks) under ktem_app_data
User database: usernames + sha256(unsalted) password hashes, admin flags
Business logic / private-vs-public collection structure revealing what an org indexed
Server filesystem via CVE-2025-53358 link-upload path traversal (e.g. ../../../../../.env) once authenticated with the default admin/admin
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
